description: Small domain coverage matrix for knowledge / ingest / runtime-projection proofs. Pair with corpus_manifest.json.

domains.intent: Curated keywords and normative language for knowledge_runtime_envelope_from_ir heuristics (network / secrets-PII / destructive) in runtime_projection.py.

runtime_projection.notes: IR knowledge_constraint summaries produced downstream should mention these themes when mapped to hard forbidden/must_not_use predicates.

domains.intent: Contradictory organizational norms to exercise mediation, ConflictDetector, and knowledge_unresolved_conflict_policy (fail_closed | warn_and_continue | defer_to_intent).

mediation.notes: Pair with intent constraints or deterministic extraction paths as in tests/integration/test_knowledge_mediation_phase2.py; newer metadata can bias tie-breaks when tests set indexed_at_ms / doc_version.

domains.intent: RFC2119 + table + numbered lines for doc-derived soft assertions when doc_derived_assertions_mode=limited (default); with doc_derived_assertions_mode=off and empty intent, extract_knowledge_snapshot returns an empty snapshot—proving opt-out disables doc-derived depth.

doc_derived_assertions.mode_off: No soft assertions from retrieved docs alone when intent has no constraints (see extract_knowledge_snapshot early return).
doc_derived_assertions.mode_limited: Soft assertions from MUST/SHOULD/BCP14-style patterns under doc_derived_max_assertions cap; compile-side patterns default via DocDerivedPatternOptions flags vs ingest DEFAULT_INGEST_DOC_DERIVED_PATTERNS in assertion_index_store.

domains.intent: Payments/PCI-style constraints to broaden proof of secrets/PII + network egress + destructive-deny projection from docs/OpenAPI/messaging sources.

runtime_projection.notes: Ensure knowledge_runtime_envelope_from_ir emits deny rules for subprocess/http and apply/rollback when summaries mention PII/secrets, external APIs, and destructive ops.

domains.intent: Migration runbooks that include destructive operations and egress constraints; ensures projection behaves outside security-network wording.

runtime_projection.notes: Migration wording like drop/wipe/purge/rm -rf should map to destructive deny rules.
